original_abstract: Readily available o-vinylphenols undergo a formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition to alkynes when treated with catalytic amounts of [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2. The reaction, which involves the cleavage of the terminal C-H bond of the alkenyl moiety, generates highly valuable benzoxepine skeletons in a practical, versatile, and atom-economical manner. Using carbon monoxide instead of an alkyne as reaction partner leads to coumarin products which formally result from a (5 + 1) cycloaddition.
humanized_abstract: Common o-vinylphenols undergo a formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition to alkynes using just a catalytic amount of the rhodium pentalenyl precursor [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2, in a process involving activation of the terminal C–H bond of the alkenyl component to produce directly useful benzoxepines in a straightforward, convenient and extremely efficient manner. By using CO instead of an alkyne, coumarins result via a formally (5 + 1) cycloaddition process.

document.output: Common o-vinylphenols undergo a formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition to alkynes using just a catalytic amount of the rhodium pentalenyl precursor [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2, in a process involving activation of the terminal C–H bond of the alkenyl component to produce directly useful benzoxepines in a straightforward, convenient and extremely efficient manner. By using CO instead of an alkyne, coumarins result via a formally (5 + 1) cycloaddition process.
